summary: Name impersonates LiveKit Agents SDK; ships trivial greet() stub
details: Package is published as `livekit-agents` and its README presents itself as the LiveKit Agents Node.js SDK, linking to docs.livekit.io and github.com/livekit/agents-js. The legitimate package is published under the `@livekit` scope as `@livekit/agents`. The actual shipped code in dist/index.js is a one-function stub that exports `greet(name)` returning `Hello, ${name}!`. There are no install/lifecycle hooks, no network calls, no filesystem reads, no credential access, and no obfuscation in the tarball. The package is a name-confusion lookalike of a widely used project but contains no installer-harm payload in this version. Confusion risk is real (developers searching for the LiveKit Agents SDK could install this stub instead), and a future release under the same name could change behavior, so human review of the name claim is appropriate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
